article_title: Impact of subanesthetic doses of ketamine on AMPA-mediated responses in rats: An in vivo electrophysiological study on monoaminergic and glutamatergic neurons

aggregated_relations.subject: PCP
aggregated_relations.object: firing rate of locus coeruleus norepinephrine neurons
evidence_sentences: Intravenous injection of PCP decreases firing rate of locus coeruleus (LC) norepinephrine (NE) neurons (Raja and Guyenet, 1980), while not modifying the firing activity of 5-HT neurons (Aghajanian et al., 1970; Raja and Guyenet, 1980).

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
aggregated_relations.object: firing of ventral tegmental area dopamine neurons
evidence_sentences: Another study demonstrated that intravenous ketamine increases firing of ventral tegmental area (VTA) dopamine (DA) neurons with potency 19 times weaker than the NMDA antagonist MK-801 (French and Ceci, 1990).
intravenous ketamine increases firing of ventral tegmental area (VTA) dopamine (DA) neurons (French and Ceci, 1990)
Another study demonstrated that intravenous ketamine increases firing of ventral tegmental area (VTA) dopamine (DA) neurons with potency 19 times weaker than the NMDA antagonist MK-801.

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Specifically, ketamine, by directly blocking NMDA receptors on gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) interneurons increases glutamate and leads to an increase in AMPA receptors activation.

chunks_extracted.section: Microiontophoresis and extracellular recording of dorsal hippocampus CA3 pyramidal neurons

aggregated_relations.subject: AMPA
evidence_sentences: Since pyramidal neurons do not discharge spontaneously in chloral hydrate anesthetized rats, a small current of AMPA (-2 to -5 nA) was constantly applied to locate those neurons, and activate them within their physiological firing range (10–15 Hz; Rank, 1975).

aggregated_relations.subject: NMDA
evidence_sentences: Drug effect was assessed by measuring the degree of excitation of pyramidal neurons (measured as number of spikes generated for 60 s ejection) induced by NMDA and AMPA applications following the acute administration of ketamine or saline.

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Despite an absence of effect of acute administration of ketamine on the rate of DA neuronal firing and bursting, the number of neurons encountered per electrode descent (population activity) increased by 113% (one-way ANOVA; F(3, 16)=5.4; p<0.01; Tukey post hoc test; n=5; Figure 2(b)) compared to vehicle-treated rats.

causal_tuples.subject: ketamine

chunks_extracted.section: Effects of acute and two-day administration of ketamine on the firing activity of LC NE neurons

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Acute administration of ketamine resulted in a significant elevation of the average rate of firing activity of NE neurons in the LC by 21% when compared to the vehicle-administered group (one-way ANOVA; F(3, 375)=4.5; p<0.05; Tukey post hoc test; n=6; Figure 3(a)).

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: In addition, acute administration of ketamine nearly doubled the proportion of NE neurons displaying burst activity (control: 17% versus ketamine: 30%; one-way ANOVA; p<0.01; F(3, 20)=9; Bonferroni post hoc test; n=6; Figure 3(b)).

chunks_extracted.section: Effects of acute ketamine administration on the responsiveness of hippocampus pyramidal neurons

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: However, a significant time interaction was obtained 30 minutes following ketamine administration (using 10 or 25 mg/kg, showing a 64% increase in AMPA-induced firing activity of pyramidal neurons compared to control rat that received saline (two-way ANOVA with repeated measures followed by Bonferroni post hoc test; F(2, 48)=6; P<0.05; Figure 4(a), (c)).

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: However, a significant increase in AMPA-evoked firing was observed 30 minutes following the higher ketamine administration compared to control rats that received saline (55% increase; two-way ANOVA followed by Bonferroni post hoc test; p<0.05; Figure 4(b), (c)).

causal_tuples.subject: ketamine
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: However, a significant time interaction was obtained 30 minutes following ketamine administration (using 10 or 25 mg/kg, showing a 64% increase in AMPA-induced firing activity of pyramidal neurons compared to control rat that received saline (two-way ANOVA with repeated measures followed by Bonferroni post hoc test; F(2, 48)=6; P<0.05; Figure 4(a), (c)).

causal_tuples.subject: ketamine
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: However, a significant increase in AMPA-evoked firing was observed 30 minutes following the higher ketamine administration compared to control rats that received saline (55% increase; two-way ANOVA followed by Bonferroni post hoc test; p<0.05; Figure 4(b), (c)).

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: In the present study ketamine administration resulted in an enhancement of AMPA- but not NMDA-evoked response in the hippocampus, suggesting that ketamine may exert a direct effect on AMPA receptors.

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Interestingly, chronic administration of ketamine resulted in a significant increase in AMPA but not NMDA receptor density in Wistar–Kyoto rats (a model for depression), whereas basal densities of these receptors were not significantly different when compared to Wistar rats (Tizabi et al., 2012).

aggregated_relations.subject: DNQX
aggregated_relations.object: surface expression of GluA1 and GluA2
evidence_sentences: Furthermore, this was coupled with an increase in surface expression of both GluA1 and GluA2 subunits of AMPA receptors, which was inhibited by an AMPA receptor antagonist 6,7-dinitroquinoxaline-2,3-dione (DNQX; Nosyreva et al., 2013).

aggregated_relations.subject: MK-801
evidence_sentences: Indeed, blockade by MK-801 of NMDA receptors located on GABA neurons leads to decreased inhibition (disinhibition) following a surge in glutamate, thus resulting in an enhancement of AMPA activation (Abdallah et al., 2014; Homayoun and Moghaddam, 2007).

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: In summary, ketamine administration resulted in an increase in catecholamine activity, and this increase was AMPA-dependent.

causal_tuples.subject: DNQX
causal_tuples.object: increase in surface expression of GluA1 and GluA2
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: Furthermore, this was coupled with an increase in surface expression of both GluA1 and GluA2 subunits of AMPA receptors, which was inhibited by an AMPA receptor antagonist 6,7-dinitroquinoxaline-2,3-dione (DNQX; Nosyreva et al., 2013).

causal_tuples.subject: chloral hydrate
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: Moreover, since chloral hydrate inhibits different types of glutamatergic receptors, it is possible that effects studied under this anesthetic are rather underestimated.

causal_tuples.subject: ketamine
causal_tuples.evidence_sentence: In summary, ketamine administration resulted in an increase in catecholamine activity, and this increase was AMPA-dependent.
